followup_notes.changes_from_prior_cycle: Maintained original assessment of 0. Added observations regarding other reviewers' handling of the missing text, notably rejecting Codex's hallucinated review.
invalid_points_from_other_reviewers: Codex hallucinates references to specific sections (e.g., 'Sec. III around Eqs. (5)-(8)') and specific figures (e.g., 'Figs. 4-5') which are nowhere in the provided bundle. Codex attempts to review content that does not exist.
valid_points_from_other_reviewers: Claude correctly identifies that the text is completely missing due to the APS paywall and refrains from certifying derivations.
Claude accurately spots duplication and volume mismatch errors in the reference list (e.g., Gibbons-Hawking volume 15 vs 17).

main_claims.claim: The cosmic horizon in an accelerating, quasi-de Sitter Universe produces stable particles that can constitute dark matter.
main_claims.notes: Cannot be evaluated because the main text of the paper is missing.

main_claims.claim: The mass of the dark matter produced via this mechanism ranges from 10 keV up to the Planck scale, depending on the equation of state and the temperature when radiation domination starts.
main_claims.notes: The calculation and results cannot be verified without the main text.

missing_assumptions.assumption: All mathematical and physical assumptions are missing from the bundle.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Without the full text, it is impossible to evaluate the validity of the assumptions made regarding the quasi-de Sitter phase, particle production rates, and freeze-in mechanics.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Can the full PDF or HTML text of the article be provided?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The scraper only captured the abstract page of the journal website instead of the full paper.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Did Reviewer Codex have access to a different bundle, or did it hallucinate the equations and figures?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: Codex references specific equations and figures not present in the provided text.

novelty_assessment: The abstract proposes an interesting mechanism for dark matter production via horizon radiation during a quasi-de Sitter phase. While particle production from horizons is a known phenomenon, applying it specifically to dark matter freeze-in in this regime could be novel. However, without the full text, a rigorous assessment of the novelty compared to existing literature cannot be made.
score_rationale: The paper receives an automatic overall score of 0 because the main text is demonstrably missing from the provided review bundle. Claude correctly noted this limitation. Codex's review contains severe hallucinations of equations and figures not present in the source text. It is impossible to verify any technical soundness or clarity without the manuscript.
summary: The provided text contains only the abstract, metadata, outline, and references for the paper; the entire main body text is missing, likely due to a paywall blocking the scraper. The abstract proposes calculating the frozen-in density of dark matter produced by the cosmic horizon in a quasi-de Sitter universe. Reviewer codex has hallucinated specific equations and section contents based on the outline, whereas Claude correctly observed the missing text. I maintain that a technical review is impossible without the actual paper.

technical_objections.evidence: The review bundle only includes the abstract, outline, and references. The actual 'Article Text' section is completely empty. Claude confirms this limitation. Codex's citations of specific equations are hallucinations.
